strategy: Buy $47/$60 Call Spread — Sep 2026 (high IV mandates spread)

wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering new CSP positions. Current environment is not ideal for premium selling.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Daily chart, 20-period 2σ Bollinger Bands. Lower BB estimated ~$40–44 at current volatility. Wait for price to tag or breach lower band before selling CSP — backtested 7/10 mean reversion. Current price $46.50 is mid-band; no entry signal yet.

csp.annualisedYield: ~35–55% annualised on capital at risk (high IV benefit)
csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured OR close 5 days before next quarterly earnings report (~May/June 2026 for Q3 FY2026). Do not hold through earnings.
csp.note: ⚠️ HIGH IV (~80–110%) — premium is rich but stock is volatile. Size to 1–2 contracts max (capital at risk: $3,800–$8,400). If assigned at $38–42, cost basis is well below current price $46.50 — assignment is acceptable but plan CC immediately.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close CC at 50% premium captured. Re-sell if stock stagnates. Close before earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned on CSP (cost basis ~$38–42), sell CC at $50–55 to collect additional premium while stock recovers. High IV makes CC premium very attractive at this level.

recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — High IV (~110%) Mandates Spread; Execution Risk High
recommendation.entryNote: HIGH IV ENVIRONMENT (~80–110% IV rank 52.9) — spread is mandatory, naked calls would be extremely expensive. Stock is at $46.50, down 40% from 52-week high of $76.87. The Microsoft $9.7B deal and NVIDIA GB300 infrastructure pivot give legitimacy, but this is a SHOW-ME story. Target: $3.4B ARR by end-2026 — ambitious. Watch for next quarterly update (IREN is Australian company, fiscal year ends June 30; Q3 FY2026 results expected ~May/June 2026). Do NOT enter immediately before any earnings report. Bernstein top pick with $100 PT, but trimmed from $125 on execution risk. Size to 1% portfolio max — capital-intensive pivot with GPU supply and financing risk. 2 spreads = $800–1,400 is within budget; adjust to 1 spread if IV is at the higher end of range.

exitPlan.label: Spread 1
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $55–58 (+18–25%) on ARR milestone update or NVIDIA GB300 delivery confirmation

exitPlan.label: Spread 2
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $60+ (+29%) on sustained GPU capacity ramp or new hyperscaler contract

companyOverview: IREN Limited (formerly Iris Energy) was a Bitcoin mining company that has executed one of the most aggressive pivots in the AI infrastructure space. Originally listed on NASDAQ as a vertically integrated data centre operator that mined Bitcoin using low-cost renewable energy in Canada and Australia, the company signed a $9.7 billion, multi-year agreement with Microsoft in November 2025 to deliver NVIDIA GB300-powered AI cloud infrastructure. This is not an LOI — it is a binding commercial agreement. The company targets $3.4B in Annual Recurring Revenue by end-2026 and currently operates 150,000 GPUs, targeting 275,000 by 2030. Bernstein analysts made IREN their top AI infrastructure pick (ahead of CRWV and NBIS) while trimming their price target from $125 to $100, citing execution risk, GPU supply delays, and higher-than-expected financing costs. The stock skyrocketed 285% in 2025 and has pulled back 40% from its 52-week high — a classic high-beta AI infrastructure pattern.

financials.note: Extreme volatility — 1,178% range. High beta stock.
Ambitious target; Q-by-Q execution updates are the key monitor
Targeting 275,000 by 2030; each GPU shipment is a catalyst
Signed Nov 2025; multi-year AI cloud infrastructure delivery
HIGH IV — spread is mandatory. Naked calls would be extremely expensive.
FY ends June 30; Q3 FY2026 results expected. WAIT for report before entering.

insiderFlow.summary: Limited Form 4 data — IREN is an Australian company (ASX + NASDAQ dual-listed); insider reporting follows Australian ASX rules primarily. Monitoring via Fintel/OpenInsider shows no significant recent Form 4 open-market activity filed in 2026.

transactions.note: Australian primary listing — insider activity tracked via ASX announcements more than SEC Form 4. No significant open-market Form 4 purchases or sales found in recent 90-day window.

insiderFlow.signal: UNKNOWN/NEUTRAL — incomplete US insider data due to Australian company structure. Monitor ASX director notices directly.

catalysts: $9.7B Microsoft binding agreement — not an LOI; this is contracted multi-year revenue with the world's largest company
NVIDIA GB300 GPU deliveries — each shipment confirmation is a re-rating catalyst; 150,000 → 275,000 GPU trajectory
$3.4B ARR target by end-2026 — quarterly updates will either confirm or pressure the thesis
AI cloud demand structural tailwind: hyperscalers cannot build fast enough; alternative GPU cloud providers fill the gap
40% pullback from 52-week high $76.87 — asymmetric risk/reward if Microsoft deal executes as contracted
Bernstein top pick: institutional research coverage with $100 price target provides floor support
risks: HIGH IV (~80–110%) — naked calls cost 20–30% of stock price; spread is mandatory per options rules
Execution risk: GPU supply delays, higher financing costs, slower-than-expected AI adoption all pressure the $3.4B ARR target
Bitcoin mining revenue declining: transition to AI cloud is underway but Bitcoin still contributes — Bitcoin price swings add noise
Capital-intensive pivot: building AI data centres at scale requires constant debt/equity raises; dilution is structural
Quarterly earnings from Australian company — timing and format differ from US companies; harder to trade around
Australian company = ASX + NASDAQ dual listing; currency risk (AUD/USD), ADR mechanics, potential delisting risk if pivot fails
Bernstein trimmed target $125 → $100: even the bull case is walking back initial optimism on execution pace

dilution.summary: ACTIVE dilution risk. Pivoting from capital-light Bitcoin mining to capital-heavy AI cloud requires significant and ongoing capital raises.

items.label: GPU infrastructure capex
items.detail: Each GB300 server rack costs $10M+. Delivering on the Microsoft contract requires billions in hardware. Must be financed via debt, equity, or asset-backed financing.

items.label: Ongoing equity raises likely
items.detail: IREN has a history of equity offerings to fund growth. Post-Microsoft deal, expect further capital raises to fund GPU fleet expansion.

items.label: Debt structure
items.detail: Higher-than-expected financing costs cited by Bernstein as a risk. Rising rates on data centre debt directly compresses economics.
